metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC5: the refspec table does not currently exist in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`; revise AC5 to say 'add a refspec table to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`' (or confirm a table location in the doc).
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 asserts 'Update the refspec table in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md', implying a refspec table already exists in that document. A full read of docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md (Step 9, Gerrit→GitHub replication section) reveals no refspec table — only prose descriptions. There is no table with refspec rows to update; the deliverable would require creating a new table, not updating an existing one.
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC5 to: 'Add a refspec table to `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` (Step 9 — Gerrit → GitHub replication section) documenting all three push refspecs: `refs/heads/main`, `refs/tags/*`, and the new `refs/changes/*`; the table did not previously exist.'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or clarify the `checkout-gerrit-change-action` / `gerrit-url` fallback claim in AC2: either confirm it is installed (zero evidence in repo) or explicitly note it is a future-story dependency and that this plan's AC2 does not rely on it being live today.
findings.finding: AC2 asserts '`checkout-gerrit-change-action`'s `gerrit-url` fallback covers replication lag' as the stated mitigation for the scenario where change-ref replication hasn't landed yet when a CI job runs. A repo-wide search for 'checkout-gerrit-change-action' and 'gerrit-url' returns zero matches anywhere in the codebase. This action is not installed, not referenced in any workflow, and not present in any config or script file. The cited fallback mechanism is entirely absent from the repo.
findings.location: AC2 / 'checkout-gerrit-change-action's gerrit-url fallback covers replication lag'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct AC-5: `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` has no refspec table — update the prose bullet list in Step 6 (§9, lines 392-395) that enumerates the push refspecs to add `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*`.
findings.finding: The plan's AC-5 references updating 'the refspec table in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md', but no such Markdown table exists in that file — the push refspecs are described only in prose bullet points (Step 6, lines 392–395). The edit target is hallucinated.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Update the refspec table in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md' with 'Update the prose refspec description in docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md §9 Step 6 (the bullet beginning "The push refspecs (`refs/heads/main…`)") to include the new `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` refspec and note the ADR-0010 reversal.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive technical rationale in the new ADR (and optionally the plan's 'Why') for why change-ref replication to GitHub is preferred over relying solely on the direct `gerrit-url` SSH-fetch fallback (e.g. Gerrit SSH is not publicly reachable from GH-hosted runners; replication avoids per-run SSH credentials on the Actions side; lower latency on the hot path).
findings.finding: The plan's 'Why' section gives a business rationale (g2p pattern, GH Actions needs to fetch the patchset) but does not state a positive technical rationale for choosing *replication to GitHub* over relying on the `checkout-gerrit-change-action` `gerrit-url` direct-fetch fallback (which the plan itself acknowledges in the AC as covering replication lag). Without this, the ADR the plan promises to write lacks a defensible 'why this over the fallback' rationale, and a future reviewer could question whether the added replication surface area was justified.
findings.location: Why section; Acceptance Criteria — second bullet ('gerrit-url fallback covers replication lag')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan (e.g., revert the refspec line and reload replication.config) to the Scope or a dedicated Rollback section, covering how to revert the replication.config change without disrupting existing heads/tags replication.
findings.finding: No rollback or back-out plan is described for the replication.config change. If refs/changes/* replication causes problems (e.g., unexpected public exposure, GitHub API rate limits, mirror performance), there is no described path to revert. T4 requires an explicit reversibility mechanism, not merely acknowledgement of the change.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' section: 'To revert: remove the refs/changes/*:refs/changes/* line from replication.config and run `ssh gerrit gerrit plugin reload replication`; existing heads/tags replication is unaffected.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate both docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md update locations in the Scope section: (a) Step 6 §9 prose refspec bullets (add refs/changes/*) and (b) the webhook-security context at line 299 (clarify that change refs now replicate but refs/meta/* still does not).
findings.finding: The prose description of push refspecs in `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` Step 6 (an outside-infra/gerrit consumer of the replication config) is not enumerated as an edit target in the plan's Scope section, only in AC-5 — and AC-5 mislabels the location as a 'table'. The same doc also contains a second stale mention at line 299 ('Step 6 keeps `replicatePermissions=false`') that describes the webhook-token protection but may need a note that change refs are now replicated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or verification step) that exercises the new path end-to-end: after merging the config change, confirm at least one `refs/changes/` ref is visible on the GitHub mirror (e.g., `git ls-remote <github-mirror-url> 'refs/changes/*'` returns at least one entry), or explicitly defer this to a named child task with rationale.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion exercises the new replication path end-to-end: the plan broadens replication to `refs/changes/*` but provides no step or AC that verifies a change ref actually appears on the GitHub mirror after the config change takes effect (e.g., 'push a patchset, confirm refs/changes/XX/YY/ZZ is visible on the GitHub remote'). AC3's 'confirm the mirror-lock ruleset still bars writes' does not substitute for this — it checks a pre-existing control, not the new behavior. Green config-file diffs alone cannot prove the replication fires and the ref lands.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all five ACs; 'What' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'After deploying the updated replication.config, run `git ls-remote <github-mirror> refs/changes/` and confirm at least one change ref is returned, verifying the replication plugin has processed the new refspec.' If a live Gerrit environment is unavailable in-session, note the deferral explicitly and name the child ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a concrete proving command for AC3, e.g., `gh api repos/<org>/<repo>/rulesets --jq '.[] | select(.name=="mirror-lock")'` returning the expected ruleset, so the check is auditable and repeatable.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Confirm the GitHub mirror-lock ruleset still bars all non-replication writes to main') uses 'confirm' without specifying the proving artifact or mechanism, leaving it effort-framed rather than observable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Confirm the GitHub mirror-lock ruleset still bars all non-replication writes to main' with 'Run `gh api repos/<org>/<repo>/rulesets` and verify the mirror-lock ruleset is present and enforced on `main`; include the output in the PR description.'}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify AC3 by specifying HOW the mirror-lock ruleset confirmation is performed (e.g., 'verify in GitHub repo settings that the branch protection/ruleset is unchanged and document the check in the PR description') and add this step to the Scope section.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Confirm the GitHub mirror-lock ruleset still bars all non-replication writes to main') describes a verification step that has no corresponding described work or deliverable in the What or Scope sections, making it an orphan criterion with no implementer guidance on HOW to perform or document this confirmation.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to: 'Verify (and document in the PR description) that the GitHub mirror-lock branch ruleset still restricts writes to main to the replication service account only — no config change required, confirmation only.' Add 'Verify mirror-lock ruleset (no change needed)' to Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability requirement: specify how replication failures for `refs/changes/*` are surfaced (e.g., Gerrit `replication_failed` log/metric alert, or a periodic canary check), so operators can detect and distinguish replication breakage from transient lag.
findings.finding: No observability instrumentation is specified for the new `refs/changes/*` replication failure point — if replication silently fails or lags beyond the fallback window, operators have no metric, log, or alert to detect it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria / Scope — no monitoring section present

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the retirement/cleanup policy for accumulated `refs/changes/*` refs on the GitHub mirror (e.g., explicitly accept unbounded accumulation as a known trade-off, or specify a periodic pruning job/GitHub ref cleanup strategy).
findings.finding: Replicated `refs/changes/*` refs accumulate on the GitHub mirror indefinitely with no retirement/cleanup path defined — every patchset ever pushed will remain as a ref in the GitHub mirror.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line ADR note: 'refs/changes/* accumulate on the mirror indefinitely; this is accepted as consistent with ONAP/O-RAN-SC practice and the mirror is not used for cloning by humans.' If not acceptable, specify a cron-based ref pruning step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC that operationally validates the new refspec works: e.g., 'after applying the config, trigger a manual replication and confirm a refs/changes/* ref is visible in the GitHub mirror repository.'
findings.finding: No AC specifies a concrete validation mechanism confirming that change refs actually appear in the GitHub mirror after replication — all criteria are config/doc deliverables with no before/after or operational signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Manually trigger replication for a test change (or wait for the next event) and confirm that `refs/changes/<XX>/<change-id>/<patchset>` appears in the GitHub mirror; record the confirmation in the PR description or a linked CI log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a live smoke-test AC: after merging, push a test patchset and confirm `git ls-remote <github-mirror> 'refs/changes/*'` returns the expected ref, validating the new replication path end-to-end before the ticket is closed.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a live behavioral cutover (change-ref replication to the GitHub mirror) but contains no acceptance criterion requiring end-to-end verification that refs/changes/* actually appear on the GitHub mirror after a real patchset push; all ACs are satisfiable by offline config edits, leaving the new replication path unvalidated in production.
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'Smoke-test: push a dummy patchset to Gerrit, wait for replication, and run `git ls-remote <github-mirror-url> refs/changes/*` to confirm the change ref is present on the GitHub mirror. Document the result in the PR/commit.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the AC (and the new ADR) whether `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` should or should not carry a `+` force-push prefix, with rationale referencing the append-only nature of Gerrit change ref slots and the re-upload edge case.
findings.finding: The plan's AC specifies adding `refs/changes/*:refs/changes/*` but is silent on whether this refspec should carry a force-push prefix (`+`). The existing `replication.config` header (line 14) treats 'no leading +' as the 'core safety property' for ALL refspecs, yet change refs have different semantics than branch refs: each Gerrit patchset creates a distinct numbered ref (refs/changes/NN/CHANGE/N), so non-force is normally safe — but a re-upload to the same patchset slot or an admin reset would make the push non-fast-forward, causing a silent failure within the retry budget and tripping the existing replication alarm. The plan leaves the implementer to guess at the correct prefix by inference, which risks either (a) silently adding `+` (weakening the mirror-lock contract) or (b) silently omitting `+` and seeing replication fail on edge-case patchset re-uploads.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: 'Add a scoped refs/changes/*:refs/changes/* refspec'
findings.suggested_fix: Since Gerrit change-ref slots are numbered per-patchset (refs/changes/NN/CHANGE/PATCHSET_NUM) and Gerrit never reuses a slot once pushed, non-force (no `+`) is correct and consistent with the existing safety property. Document this explicitly in the AC: 'Do NOT add a + prefix — change ref slots are numbered and append-only, so non-force replication is correct; a push failure on re-upload (if it ever occurs) is the intended safe-failure behaviour.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
